items.title: Transparency data: DBT: workforce management information January 2026
items.summary: Reports on departmental staff numbers and costs.

items.title: Official Statistics: Warm Homes: Social Housing Fund statistics - March 2026
items.summary: Data to monitor the installation of energy efficiency measures in domestic properties via the Warm Homes: Social Housing Fund scheme in England.

items.title: Official Statistics: Warm Homes: Local Grant statistics - March 2026
items.summary: Data to monitor the installation of energy efficiency measures in domestic properties via the Warm Homes: Local Grant scheme in England.

items.title: Statistics at DESNZ
items.summary: We publish data and statistics on emissions, energy sources, prices and efficiency, fuel poverty, and public attitudes towards energy policy.

items.title: Guidance: Standards for official statistics published by DESNZ
items.summary: Documents describing the standards we follow to produce official statistics.

items.title: Official Statistics: DESNZ Public Attitudes Tracker: Summer 2026
items.summary: This regular survey collects data on public awareness, behaviours and attitudes relating to the department’s policy areas. These include Net Zero and climate change, different types of energy infrastructure, energy use in the home and energy bills.

items.title: Corporate report: North Sea Transition Authority (Oil and Gas Authority) Review 2025
items.summary: The government is required by the Energy Act 2016 to conduct a review of the Oil and Gas Authority, business name North Sea Transition Authority (NSTA), at least every 3 years.

items.title: Guidance: Domestic energy tariff reductions 2026: guidance for energy suppliers
items.summary: Guidance for energy suppliers on how to apply discounts to domestic tariffs from 1 April 2026, related to the RO to Exchequer scheme and closure of the ECO and GBIS schemes.

items.title: Oil and gas: offshore environmental legislation
items.summary: Environmental regulations and guidance on offshore oil and gas exploration and production offshore gas unloading and storage and offshore carbon dioxide storage activities

items.title: Form: Climate Change Agreements Scheme: templates for Umbrella and Underlying agreements
items.summary: These templates are current and supersede previous templates.

items.title: Accredited official statistics: Weekly road fuel prices
items.summary: Road fuel price statistics providing average UK retail 'pump' prices on a weekly basis.

items.title: Electricity network infrastructure: consents, land access and rights
items.summary: We are seeking views on proposed changes to land rights and consents processes for electricity network infrastructure.

items.title: Evolution of economic regulation for CO2 storage
items.summary: We’re seeking views and evidence on economic regulation for CO2 storage to guide policy and support growth within an evolving carbon capture, usage and storage (CCUS) sector.

items.title: Form: UK Emissions Trading Scheme and Carbon Price Support: apply for compensation
items.summary: How to apply for compensation for the indirect costs of the UK Emissions Trading Scheme (UK ETS) and the Carbon Price Support (CPS) mechanism.

items.title: Form: UK Emissions Trading Scheme and Carbon Price Support: apply for compensation
items.summary: How to apply for compensation for the indirect costs of the UK Emissions Trading Scheme (UK ETS) and the Carbon Price Support (CPS) mechanism.

items.title: Transparency data: DBT: workforce management information March 2026
items.summary: Reports on departmental staff numbers and costs.

items.title: UK Trade Tariff: duty suspensions and autonomous tariff quotas
items.summary: Temporary duty suspensions and autonomous tariff quotas (ATQs) for importing goods into the UK.

items.title: Notice: Heat Pump Ready Programme Round 2: innovation funding competition
items.summary: Apply to the Heat Pump Ready Programme Round 2 innovation funding competition.

items.title: Accredited official statistics: Solar PV deployment: August 2026
items.summary: Monthly deployment of solar photovoltaic capacity in the United Kingdom.

items.title: Accredited official statistics: Solar PV deployment: May 2026
items.summary: Monthly deployment of solar photovoltaic capacity in the United Kingdom.

items.title: Jet fuel and travel plans: what you need to know
items.summary: UK airlines say that they are not currently seeing a shortage of jet fuel. The government is working with industry and international partners to keep passengers moving.

items.title: Guidance: Security & Policing 2026: countries, territories and organisations invited to attend by the Department for Business and Trade
items.summary: Countries, territories and organisations invited by the Department for Business and Trade to attend Security & Policing 2026.

items.title: Access your UK Kyoto Protocol Registry account
items.summary: The UK National Registry (Kyoto Protocol Registry) is now closed.

items.title: Transparency data: Ministerial Group for Digital Inclusion: terms of reference
items.summary: Terms of reference for Ministerial Group for Digital Inclusion.

items.title: Transparency data: Ministerial Group for Digital Inclusion: meeting summaries
items.summary: Summary of minutes of the meetings for the Ministerial Group for Digital Inclusion.

items.title: Notice: CCUS East Coast Cluster: NPT Pathfinder selection process
items.summary: Apply to the NPT Pathfinder selection process for non-pipeline transport CCUS projects seeking ECC Teesside network connection by 2032.

items.title: Standard individual export licences (SIELs)
items.summary: You can use a SIEL to make shipments of specified military or dual-use items to a named consignee and/or end-user.

items.title: Trade sanctions, arms embargoes, and other trade restrictions
items.summary: Information on trade sanctions, arms embargoes and trade restrictions, including trade controls, transit controls and restrictions on terrorist organisations.

items.title: UK strategic export controls
items.summary: The UK's regulatory framework for strategic export controls and the circumstances where you might need an export licence.

items.title: Guidance: Applying for export licences using SPIRE
items.summary: How to use the online export licensing system to get a licence to export controlled goods, software and technology overseas.

items.title: Apply for a licence to carry out sanctioned trade through OTSI
items.summary: Check if you need a licence from the Office for Trade Sanctions Implementation (OTSI), and apply online.

items.title: Accredited official statistics: Energy trends and prices: February 2026
items.summary: Monthly production, trade, electricity generation and consumption of coal, electricity, gas, oil and total energy; retail price data, petrol & diesel price data and EU comparative price data.

items.title: Official Statistics: Strategic export controls: licensing statistics: 1 October to 31 December 2025
items.summary: This statistics release will provide data about decisions made on licence applications to the Export Control Joint Unit (ECJU) of the Department for Business and Trade (DBT) for 1 October to 31 December 2025 (2025 Q4). From the 2024 Q1 publication until further notice, these statistics have been reclassified and published as Official Statistics in Development.

items.title: Trading under sanctions with Russia
items.summary: What import and export restrictions apply due to sanctions for UK persons, including UK businesses and UK natural persons, when trading with Russia.

items.title: How to apply for a trade sanctions licence
items.summary: Find out which body is responsible for licensing your activity, and send your application to the correct team.

items.title: How OTSI assesses applications for trade sanctions licences
items.summary: This guidance is for applications submitted to OTSI seeking licences to carry out sanctioned activity related to both services and goods.

items.title: Guidance: Horizon Convictions Redress Scheme (HCRS): legal cost framework
items.summary: This framework sets out the legal costs we will cover for those applying for financial redress under the Horizon Convictions Redress Scheme (HCRS).

items.title: Guidance: Horizon Convictions Redress Scheme (HCRS): assessment framework
items.summary: This assessment framework sets out how we will assess your claim if you request a detailed assessment under the Horizon Convictions Redress Scheme (HCRS).

items.title: Horizon Convictions Redress Scheme (HCRS): alternative dispute resolution (ADR)
items.summary: Guidance on how to appeal your offer under the Horizon Convictions Redress Scheme (HCRS).

items.title: Horizon Convictions Redress Scheme (HCRS): request a detailed assessment
items.summary: Use this guidance and form if you are requesting a detailed assessment under the Horizon Convictions Redress Scheme (HCRS).
